creator: gperciva@tarsnap.com
text:
Thanks!

SEE ALSO looks good.

For the -a part, I'd be tempted to leave the first paragraph unchanged -- that is, keep the mention of -T in there.  In the proposed version, the -T is way at the bottom and thus easier to overlook.

I think we can omit the "assuming big-endian ordering" -- as per comment 1, the implementation of rpcinfo in FreeBSD already assumes big-endian.

Also, I'd be tempted to remove the mention of the hex address.  I think that everybody is familiar with the form 192.168.1.125.

As a more general note, I think this is ready for Phabricator.  (That said, I'm not a FreeBSD committer or maintainer, so take my opinions with a grain of salt!)
